zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc.於1034年出生在法國諾門迪艾布利奧安時，他的父親Anquetil d'Harcourt, Seigneur d'Harcourt45歲，母親Eve de Boissey, Dame de Boissey-le-Châtel26歲。他與Colette d'Argouges在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1089年住在法國豪-諾門迪艾哈爾庫特。他去世於大約1106年在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾牛頓 哈爾庫特享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: etc.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Hiroaki, Miho, Hirohiko, Hirokazu, Junichi, Kazuo, Keiji, Kenta, Masaji, Masazumi, Nami, Seiji.

Japanese (Etō): written 江藤 ‘inlet wisteria’. It is found mainly in western Japan. Despite the element 藤 -tō (also pronounced fuji) in the name, bearers are descended from the Taira clan rather than from the Fujiwara .
West African (Nigeria): from a personal name found among the Ibibio and Efik peoples, meaning ‘tree’.

variants.name: Taira
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Isamu, Kenji, Minoru, Tsutomu, Yoshinori, Aiko, Fumiko, Haruo, Hatsu, Hiromi, Hiromitsu, Hiroshi.

Japanese: written 平 ‘peace’ or ‘level’. Together with the Fujiwara and Minamoto , this is one of the most prominent clans in Japanese history. It is listed in the Shinsen shōjiroku. The name appears to have been taken from Taira no Miyako (‘Capital of Peace’), an ancient epithet for the city of Kyōto. The Taira are also commonly called Heike 平家 or Heishi 平氏, from the Sino-Japanese pronunciation of Taira + the words for family or clan.

History: The name Taira was created in 825 by the ex-Emperor Kanmu when his son Takamune (804–867) became a commoner. Later emperors bestowed the name on some of their children, so the various branches of the clan were distinguished by the names of their parent-emperors. The Taira clan was briefly paramount in Japan in the late 12th century and its fall during a war with the Minamoto is told in the great poetic epic Heike monogatari ‘Tales of the Taira’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: "Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Fort"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the common surname, in origin a local name for someone who lived near a place where a river could be crossed by wading through it (Old English ford).

nameMeanings.name: d'Harcourt,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: Originally an Anglicized spelling of the German name   Kurt  , but now also used as a short form of   Curtis .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc. was born in 1034, in Brionne, Eure, Normandy, France, his father, Anquetil d'Harcourt, Seigneur d'Harcourt, was 45 and his mother, Eve de Boissey, Dame de Boissey-le-Châtel, was 26. He married Colette d'Argouges in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Harcourt, Eure, Upper Normandy, France in 1089. He died about 1106, in Newton Harcourt, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc. est né en 1034, à Brionne, Eure, Normandie, France, son père, Anquetil d'Harcourt, Seigneur d'Harcourt, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Eve de Boissey, Dame de Boissey-le-Châtel, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Colette d'Argouges à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Harcourt, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France en 1089. Il est décédé environ 1106, à Newton Harcourt, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc.が1034年にフランスノーマンディエウアブリオンで生まれた時，父Anquetil d'Harcourt, Seigneur d'Harcourtは45歳，母Eve de Boissey, Dame de Boissey-le-Châtelは26歳であった。イギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアでColette d'Argougesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1089年にはフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアハーコウルトに住んでいた。彼は約1106年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアニュートン ハーコウルトで73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc.(이)가 1034년 프랑스 노먼디 우르 브리오니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Anquetil d'Harcourt, Seigneur d'Harcourt(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve de Boissey, Dame de Boissey-le-Châtel(은)는 26세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 Colette d'Argouges(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1089년도에 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 하르쿠르트에서 살았다. 그는 대략 1106년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 뉴턴 하르쿠르트에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc. родился в 1034 г. в местности брионн, эур, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Anquetil d'Harcourt, Seigneur d'Harcourt было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Eve de Boissey, Dame de Boissey-le-Châtel, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Colette d'Argouges в местности лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности харкоурт, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция в 1089 году. Он умер около 1106 г. в местности нютон харкоурт, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет.
